questions.question: Who inspired the global phenomenon known as the “ice bucket challenge” to tackle a deadly neurodegenerative disease?
questions.explanation: A former US college baseball player who helped inspire the global phenomenon known as the “ice bucket challenge” to tackle a deadly neurodegenerative disease has died at the age of 34.A one-time college athlete from the Boston area, Pete Frates’ struggle with ALS, also known as Lou Gehrig’s disease, was one of the inspirations behind the ice bucket challenge which took social media by storm in 2014.

questions.question: A National level Business meet was organized by CPWD in which state?
questions.explanation: The Central Public Works Department (CPWD) organized a Business Meet with the Clients and stakeholders on 11 December 2019 in New Delhi. It aimed to share and familiarize the important activities and achievements of the Department. The national-level meeting was held to get the views from the Clients and stakeholders about the Services provided by CPWD. The meeting showcased a rejuvenated CPWD through a process of all-inclusiveness and wider consultations.

questions.question: Who won the Breakthrough sports person award at 2019 FICCI India Sports Awards?
questions.explanation: Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI) India Sports Awards 2019 was presented to the Railway Sports Promotion Board under the category of Best Company Promoting Sports (Public Sector) at New Delhi. The award was conferred by a jury headed by Justice Mukul Mudgal, retired Chief Justice of Punjab and Haryana High Court. Best National Sports Federation: National Rifle Association of India. Sports person (female): Rani Rampal (hockey). Sports person (male): Saurabh Chaudhary (shooting). Para-athlete: Sandeep Chaudhary (javelin). Breakthrough sports person: Amit Panghal (boxing). Lifetime achievement (administrator): Govindaraj Kempareddy. Lifetime achievement: Pankaj Advani (cue sport). Best State promoting sports: Odisha. Best sports journalist: Kamesh Srinivasan.

questions.question: Legislative Assembly of which state has recently passed bills to create two separate commissions for SCs and STs?
questions.explanation: The Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly passed two Bills, namely Andhra Pradesh State Commission for Scheduled Tribes Bill and Andhra Pradesh State Commission for Scheduled Castes Bill on 16 December 2019. The two bills were passed by voice note. The Bills will enable to set up the separate Commissions for Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs). The Andhra Pradesh State Commission for ST Bill was tabled by the State Minister for Tribal Welfare Pushpa Srivani. It will allow setting up of separate Commissions to safeguard the interests of STs.

questions.question: Baburmahal Gram Panchayat of which state has got best performance award in Rural Job Scheme awards?
questions.explanation: West Bengal has got the first prize in 100-day work programme for promoting livelihoods. State’s Bankura and Cooch Behar stood first and second respectively among districts in the country in implementation of the rural job scheme. Baburmahal GP (gram panchayat) of Kulpi, South 24 Paraganas also got the best performance award.

questions.question: Which international finance institution is funding the Khyber Pass Economic Corridor (KPEC) project of Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Pakistan and the World Bank have signed a loan agreement worth USD 406 million for the Khyber Pass Economic Corridor (KPEC) project. The project is expected to create more than 100,000 jobs in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province. The project aims to construct of a 48-km long, four-lane, 7.3- meter-wide dual carriageway, high speed access-controlled motorway, which will run from Peshawar to Torkham border point with Afghanistan, in a bid to promote economic development and uplift areas adjoining the expressway and falling in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP).

questions.question: Five US tech giants are currently facing lawsuits over the death of child labourers in cobalt mines in which country?
questions.explanation: Apple, Microsoft and Google’s parent Alphabet are among the tech giants who were named in a case that is currently looking into the death of child labourers in cobalt mines in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Democratic Republic of Congo is the largest producer of Cobalt, a rare metal and a vital raw material for batteries used in mobile phones and electric vehicles.

questions.question: Which city is host to World’s First Global Refugee Forum?
questions.explanation: The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) is a United Nations (UN) agency, with an aim to assist and protect refugees. The first ever Global Refugee Forum is being conducted by the UNHCR on 17 and 18 December 2019 in Geneva, Switzerland. The Ministerial level event will be co-hosted by UNHCR and Switzerland . It is co-convened by the country heads of Turkey, Ethiopia, Germany and Costa Rica. Pakistan’s Prime Minister Imran Khan also co-convenes the forum.

questions.question: The Abraham Koshy Committee was constituted for which organisation?
questions.explanation: The Abraham Koshy Committee is an eight member panel that serves as an advisory body for the SEBI/ Securities and Exchange Board of India. It was recently revamped to look into the issues of investor education and protection. It is headed by Abraham Koshy who is a former professor of IIM Ahmedabad.

questions.question: President Miguel Diaz Canel is reintroduce the post of Prime Minister in his country after more than 4 decades. What country is he the president of?
questions.explanation: The Cuba government is to get the office of Prime Minister after more than 40 years. The last Prime Minister of the Central American country was Fidel Castro who abolished the post after becoming the president. The Cuban Prime Minister is to be the head of the government with a 5 year term.
